LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SECOND, FOURTH, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO PAY OVERTIME WAGES IN FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE AMOUNT OF WAGES THAT REMAIN UNPAID, WHEN PLAINTIFF DISCHARGED OR QUIT AND WHEN RECEIVED LAST PAYCHECK DUE, TO PLEAD SPECIFIC FACTS SUPPORTING THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, AND DEMONSTRATE COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 382. GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO PAY REGULAR WAGES. THE COURT ADDS: 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: ANN MENDOZA, CSR #6812

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER MOOT AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, FAILURE TO PAY REGULAR WAGES IN VIEW OF COURT'S RULING ON MOTION TO STRIKE. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (FAILURE TO PROVIDE REST AND MEAL BREAKS) BECAUSE COURT PREVIOUSLY GRANTED MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (B&P SEC. 17200). PLAINTIFF MUST COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 382 TO BRING REPRESENTATIVE ACTION UNDER B&P SEC. 17200. ABSENT CLASS CERTIFICATION, RELIEF AND RESTITUTION CANNOT EXTEND BEYOND THE NAMED PLAINTIFF. HARPER VS. 24 HOUR FITNESS, INC. (2008) 167 CAL.APP. 4TH 966. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer moot as to 1st cause of action, failure to pay regular wages in view of court's ruling on motion to strike. Sustained without leave to amend as to 3rd cause of action (failure to provide rest and meal breaks) because court previously granted motion for judgment on the pleadings without leave to amend. Sustained with one final leave to amend as to 5th cause of action (B&P sec. 17200). Plaintiff must comply with CCP section 382 to bring representative action under B&P sec. 17200. Absent class certification, relief and restitution cannot extend beyond the named plaintiff. Harper vs. 24 Hour Fitness, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App. 4th 966. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings And Req. For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Dec-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Appearances: Jason Lundberg, Esq. of Ladva Law Firm (415) 296-8844, appearing for Plaintiff; Dean Royer, Esq. & Jessica P. Albert, Esq. of Siegel & Yee (510) 839-1200 appearing for Defendant. (This tentative ruling as amended is not being contested.) The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Granted with thirty days leave to amend as to second, fourth, and fifth causes of action, and as to plaintiff's claim for failure to pay overtime wages in first cause of action. Plaintiff must allege amount of wages that remain unpaid, when plaintiff discharged or quit and when received last paycheck due, to plead specific facts supporting the fourth cause of action, and demonstrate compliance with CCP section 382. Granted without leave to amend as to third cause of action and plaintiff's claim for failure to pay regular wages. The Court adds: 6th cause of action is granted without leave to amend. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Ann Mendoza CSR # 6812
